For a fixed system S on a fixed domain, the admissible interior is unique up to extensional, or lawful, equivalence. verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 2.8 (What is not assumed at the compositional layer). No extra admissibility predicate, no separate standing classifier, no metric structure, and no specific deductive formalism are inserted as independent structural data at the compositional layer. That omission is not a search for a deeper generator beneath admissibility, standing, reference, or irreversibility. Within the present manuscript, Theorems 4.29 to 4.31 show that any purported lower generator either imports illicit structure, collapses to triviality, violates the base assumptions, or reduces extensionally to the admissibility gate itself, and that a same-domain extension cannot evade that conclusion without either importing fresh authority for the old acts, violating the base goods, or collapsing back to the gate extensionally. Primitive status here therefore means structural non-derivability within the same fixed-domain meaningful regime rather than merely undeveloped. The present base is minimal on the compositional side, not a sub-kernel from which the governance layer itself is produced.

A variation may receive several dispositions; closure requires coverage, not exclusive assignment.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Theorem P.3 (Assignment-space forcing theorem). On the fixed quantum scope, the assignment space ARef is not an optional modeling layer or discretionary representational convenience. Any admissible same-domain realization of the target explanatory burden induces, up to lawful samescope equivalence, a canonical record-fixation assignment structure. Proof. The target burden is not merely to list physically realized post-measurement histories, but to support same-domain reuse of measurement records. For each construction token d ∈ D, the theory must therefore determine which physically realized branch-record pair functions as the standing realization of d. If no such determination exists, the target burden fails. If the determination is supplied by an additional same-scope selector, classifier, or admissibility-relevant parameter not fixed by the original quantum scope, then a second same-scope gate or selector has been introduced, which Part V excludes. If the burden is changed instead, one has changed the theorem object rather than sharpened its treatment. Hence every admissible same-domain realization induces a canonical assignment structure on the fixed domain.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 5.10 (Why endpoint certification does not collapse the theory). Theorem 5.6 does not reduce coherence to ordinary endpoint evaluation in the usual static sense, and it should not be read as a theorem about arbitrary path theories. The certifying endpoint predicate is not mere consistency, utility, agreement, or any other external terminal feature. It is standing, and standing is constrained here by admissibility, tensor content, and irrecoverability on the fixed substrate. The theorem is compressive only because the regime is history-sensitive: once failure is irrecoverable, terminal standing already carries the effect of the admissible history leading to that endpoint. In that sense the endpoint criterion is not a weakening of the trajectory theory but one of its sharpest signatures.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 2.12 (Latent-but-non-witnessed structure has no same-scope standing). A latent variable, metadata layer, hidden label, or broader-theory parameter that is not representable by an invariant witness in the declared scope, and that does not alter the standing quotient or admissible transport/history structure, defines no distinct same-scope standing state. If such structure becomes visible only after enlarging the admitted tests, changing the transport interface, or adding new load, then the construction has left the fixed-domain comparison class. This remains so even for an arbitrarily small scope extension: witnessability obtained by changing the admitted tests is a typed domain change, not a same-scope completion. Thus latent existence outside the witness ledger is not a same-scope extension; it is either skin for the present domain or a typed larger-domain construction. If it is retained as identity-bearing while remaining unwitnessed inside the declared domain, it is hidden unwitnessed structure and therefore a standing-failure mode under Definition 2.53.
canonical_formulation: Latent-but-non-witnessed structure has no same-scope standing

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 6.3 (Outside-envelope transformations are not same-scope transformations). A transformation outside AdL may be mathematically legitimate and may define a valuable larger-domain construction. It is not, however, a same-scope transformation unless it preserves standing, transport completeness, and fixed-domain comparability. If it fails any one of these, it is outside the audit target; if it preserves all of them, Theorem 6.2 forces it either to factor through the normal form or to be expressible as a certificate-bearing finite same-domain composition. Thus outside-AdL status is not by itself an objection: it is a demand to specify which fixed-domain clause remains preserved and to provide the corresponding transport certificate.
canonical_formulation: Outside-envelope transformations are not same-scope transformations

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Lemma 8.7 (Hybrid reduction). Let H ⊆ {C1, C2, C3, C4} be the nonempty set of classes instantiated by a successful resolution attempt. Then H has a unique priority-maximal element. All lower-priority components are either implementation details of that dominant mode or redundant once the dominant mode is present. Therefore no second-order hybrid supplies a fifth same-scope resolution class. Proof. The admissibility-priority preorder is linear on the four classes, so every nonempty finite subset has a unique maximum. If C4 is present, the attempt has already left or reset the fixed domain, and lower-priority internal features no longer decide a same-domain branch. If C3 is maximal, the branch-relevant authority is an external parameter; any canonical rule or invariant asymmetry merely processes that imported datum. If C2 is maximal, the decisive ingredient is an authorized section; C1-level features, if present, are redundant for the classification. If only C1 is present, invariant asymmetry is the resolution. Hence hybrids reduce to one dominant locus and do not open a new class.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Definition 2.5 (Governed construction and non-degenerate construction). Let X be a target object on a fixed domain D. A governed construction of X, written Constr(X), is a raw step-sequence on D that preserves, at each step, determinate reference to X, standing of the step as an admissible construction step, and irreversibility of inadmissible steps. A governed construction is non-degenerate iff those three conditions hold together on the same fixed domain. A regime is degenerate relative to the present target iff it may still compute, transform, symbolize, or produce outputs but fails to preserve target identity, admissible stephood, act-time finality, or redescription fidelity as conditions of determinate same-domain construction. This definition is classificatory rather than generative: it does not assume that all raw step-sequences already count as governed constructions, but distinguishes exactly those sequences for which admissibility-assessable construction is well-defined on the fixed domain. Admissibility is not a fifth ingredient placed beside reference, standing, and irreversibility; it names the governance boundary under which those three conditions are jointly enforceable on the same sequence.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 0.3 (What “closure by exhaustion” means in this manuscript). In this paper, “closure by exhaustion” does not mean that many candidate alternatives were surveyed informally. It means something narrower and stronger: any same-scope admissibility-bearing modification must enter through a fixed set of local loci, and no modification survives once those loci are separately closed. In the front matter this appears as the fixed comparison class of Definition 0.1; in the body it appears as act-level singleton closure, quotient / role factorization, and history-level factorization; and in the final scope note it appears as the counterexample burden. The exhaustion claim is therefore structural: a reader who wishes to reopen the space must identify a same-scope admissibility-bearing modification that does not alter any of the locally closed loci.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: 4.31. Lemma 4A (Non-generation of ATS structure) The anchor / tensor / skin decomposition introduces no new admissibility-relevant structure. In particular, it defines no new invariants, operators, or admissibility criteria, but only re-expresses structure already forced by admissibility, standing, redescription invariance, and fail-closed enforcement. Explanatory compression, earlier visibility of already-forced continuations, or derivational convenience does not constitute generation of new admissibilitybearing structure. Proof. Anchors correspond to preconditions of admissibility already fixed by the standing/admissibility kernel. Tensors correspond to quotient-level invariants and compatibility relations already forced by the standing-preserving identity relation. Skin corresponds to representatives of those classes. No new admissibility-bearing object is added; the role discipline only factors pre-existing structure into its fixed-scope loci. Apparent inferential gain produced by the decomposition is therefore epistemic compression rather than structural generation. □

Tensors correspond to quotient-level invariants and compatibility relations already forced by the standing-preserving identity relation. Skin corresponds to representatives of those classes. Lemma 0B (Concatenation closure is forced by compositional admissibility) Treating histories as finite sequences introduces no new operator and no execution: it is the structural form of admissible sequencing. In particular, if admissible composition/sequencing is a typed object of admissibility (composition level), then the space of histories must be closed under finite concatenation: for any histories h, σ ∈ Act<ω , the concatenation h · σ is again a history object. Proof. Composition and sequencing are defined as ordered chaining relations on admitted envelopes/acts. An “ordered chain” is, structurally, a finite sequence. If the system refused closure under concatenation, then the admissibility question “is the composite chain h followed by σ legal?” would be ill-typed (its subject would not exist as a structural object). That collapses the composition level by undefinedness. Therefore concatenation closure is not an extra assumption: it is forced by the existence of compositional admissibility questions. □

D is pure skin: it distinguishes representatives inside the same standing class and is therefore not constant on ∼S -classes. That makes detectability representation-dependent.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Remark 12.7 (Relation to quantum no-signaling). This theorem does not derive the quantum no-signaling equalities from Hilbert-space dynamics. Quantum no-signaling remains an interior formal result of the quantum regime. This also does not deny Bell’s theorem or replace its locality assumptions. Bell-locality, parameter independence, and outcome independence are interior formal conditions on probability models. The theorem says only that a boundary-level account of joint standing cannot itself add a controllable signaling channel behind or beneath those formal results. The exhaustion question is whether a controllable boundary channel survives no-operator, no-selector, no-parameter, and no-standing-transfer constraints; it does not.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Theorem M.4 (Invariant-measure insufficiency). Consider a symmetric branching event with indistinguishable branches ρ1 , ρ2 in the sense above. Let μ be any admissible-redescription-invariant measure on Sphys . Then μ alone cannot define a singular continuation rule without introducing additional selector structure. Proof. Since ρ1 and ρ2 are related by admissible redescription, any invariant observable assigns equal values to them (Lemma M.2). If μ is invariant, it assigns equal weight to the equivalence class containing ρ1 and ρ2 (or equal density in symmetric cases). Therefore μ does not distinguish between ρ1 and ρ2 . A singular continuation rule requires selecting one branch as the unique outcome-relevant continuation. Any such selection must either: 1. break admissible-redescription invariance (violating AER stability), or 2. introduce additional data not determined by the reduced state (meta-selection under AER-3). Hence an invariant measure is insufficient to produce singular continuation.

verbatim_or_near_verbatim_claim: Theorem 3.9 (Admissible physical content is invariant content). Within the fixed gauge-sector comparison class, a same-object claim has admissible physical content only through redescriptioninvariant tensor or observable data. Hence an overlap-level forced-coincidence result is not merely notational: it is uniqueness of the admissible physical content in that comparison class. Any alleged physical difference between admissibly redescribed representatives is either an invariant tensor difference, in which case the same-object hypothesis has failed, or a skin difference, in which case it has no physical standing. Proof. Suppose two representatives are related by admissible redescription. By Definition 3.1, anchor roles and tensor compatibility are preserved and all differences are confined to skin. If a purported physical claim distinguishes the two representatives despite that preservation, the distinction must either depend on a redescription-variant label or on added invariant structure. In the first case, Theorem 3.6 blocks the distinction as standing-bearing content. In the second case, the added invariant changes the tensor package or standing quotient and therefore exits the same-object comparison. Thus admissible physical content is exactly the invariant content available
